cross_scene_arcs.title: From Detached Witness to Committed Adoptive Father
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: John transitions from passive observer during a traumatic birth to actively pursuing adoption as a path to rebuild family, culminating in emotional investment in Esther as a daughter.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: present but detached during traumatic birth
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: emotionally invested in adopting Esther and established as a nurturing parental figure
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: how his idealized vision of family aligns with Esther's true nature

cross_scene_arcs.title: Growing Parenting Conflict and Divergence from Kate
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: John and Kate's relationship deteriorates as disagreements over parenting escalate, shifting from tension to estrangement and ultimately adversarial confrontation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: increased tension with Kate over parenting approach
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: relationship with Kate becomes adversarial, with John assuming authority over custody decisions
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Kate's concerns about Esther are dismissed, prioritizing perceived stability over investigation

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting Esther's Danger and Moral Disintegration
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: John moves from suspicion about Esther's past to reluctant acceptance, then shock at her inappropriate advance, ending in his murder by her.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: enters state of moral conflict about adoption
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: deceased, killed by Esther after rejecting her romantic advance
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Esther's manipulation and violent intent were not fully recognized or acted upon in time

cross_scene_arcs.title: Establishment and Collapse of Family Authority
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: John rises as the central decision-maker in rebuilding the family through adoption, asserts authority during crisis, but loses control as his judgment is undermined by Esther's actions and his own denial.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: shifts from physical intimacy attempt to emotional support and adoption advocacy
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: deceased, his authority dismantled by the child he sought to protect
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: his failure to recognize threats within the home compromised the safety he aimed to ensure

cross_scene_arcs.title: Trauma and Fragmented Motherhood
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Kate's experience of childbirth trauma evolves into a fractured understanding of motherhood, marked by grief, denial, and eventual integration of loss into family life.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: in acute psychological shock after contradictory birth experience and false death announcement
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: articulates grief over baby Jessie by framing her as an angel, integrating loss into family narrative
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: the nature of the infant presented at birth and its relation to Jessie remains unclear

cross_scene_arcs.title: Path to Adoption and Emotional Reconnection
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Kate moves from emotional numbness and resistance to cautious openness about adopting a child, culminating in her physical assumption of caregiving for Esther.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: emotionally withdrawn, fearful of failing as a mother again
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: commits to raising Esther, transitioning into a new maternal role
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether adopting Esther honors or erases the memory of Yolanda

cross_scene_arcs.title: Growing Suspicion and Unraveling Esther's Identity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Kate's initial curiosity about Esther's private life escalates into suspicion as she uncovers falsified documents and institutional ties, leading to a violent confrontation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: notices a photo in Esther's Bible, beginning to observe her closely
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: kills Esther in self-defense after recognizing her as a lethal threat
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: the significance of 'saarne Instituut' and its possible connection to Kate's father remains unexplained

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mother-Daughter Bonds Amid Loss
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Kate's caregiving relationship with Max evolves from routine nurture to protective action, though her focus on external threats overlooks Max's internal trauma.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: actively parenting Max, establishing daily bond
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: protects Max from Esther's attack but fails to recognize her daughter's psychological injury
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Max's trauma goes unnoticed and unaddressed by Kate

cross_scene_arcs.title: Trauma, Adoption, and Transition to the Coleman Family
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Esther is identified as a high-risk adoptee due to unexplained trauma and behavioral issues, then officially leaves the orphanage to join the Coleman family.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: perceived as high-risk adoptee with severe trauma
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: officially adopted and in transit to new home
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Sister Abigail's troubled expression suggests doubt about the stability or wisdom of the adoption

cross_scene_arcs.title: Escalation of Violence and Control Through Fear
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Esther responds to bullying and perceived threats with escalating violence, using intimidation, weapons, and murder to assert dominance and eliminate threats.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: personal safety violated and trauma response triggered
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: deceased after attempting to kill Kate and Max
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Max is left in a state of visible trauma that Kate does not recognize

cross_scene_arcs.title: Unmasking and Collapse of the False Identity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Esther's true identity as an adult imposter is exposed, leading to the violent destruction of her fabricated childhood and culminating in her death.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: exposes physical markers of her true age and trauma
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: deceased at the scene from two gunshot wounds
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Max is left in a state of visible trauma that Kate does not recognize

cross_scene_arcs.title: Relationships Forged Through Coercion and Fear
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Esther establishes and maintains control over Max and Daniel through intimidation, violence, and manipulation, turning familial bonds into instruments of fear.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: relationship with Brenda becomes overtly hostile and violent
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: final interaction with Max involves physical attack and being struck by her
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Daniel may still pose a risk of exposure despite current compliance
